title: TraderSync Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)
meta_title: TraderSync Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026) | TradingToolsHub
meta_description: TraderSync Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026). TraderSync (4.4★, $29.95/mo). All plans compared with hidden fees revealed. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete breakdown of TraderSync pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
TraderSync Pricing Overview

TraderSync is an AI-powered trading journal built for active traders across stocks, options, futures, forex, and crypto. Founded in 2018 and based in Austin, Texas, the platform has built a reputation for combining detailed journaling with conversational AI analysis through its Cypher AI assistant.

TraderSync operates on a subscription-only model with no free tier. The platform starts at $29.95 per month for the Pro plan and scales to $79.95 per month for the Elite tier. Unlike some competitors, there's no free trial or lite version—you commit to a paid plan from day one, though most users report satisfaction with the value at the entry level.

All TraderSync Plans Compared

TraderSync offers three distinct pricing tiers, each designed for different trader profiles and trading volumes:

Plan Monthly Price Key Features Best For
Pro $29.95/mo Journaling, broker integration (single account), Cypher AI (limited messages), mobile app, basic analytics Beginner day traders, swing traders testing the platform
Premium $49.95/mo Multiple account support (up to 10), Market replay (1-second precision), enhanced Cypher AI (higher message limits), advanced analytics, trade statistics Active day traders, options traders, traders managing multiple accounts
Elite $79.95/mo Up to 20 accounts, unlimited Cypher AI messages, priority support, custom reporting, risk management tools, all Premium features Prop firm traders, high-volume traders, trading teams

Annual Billing Discount: While the tool data doesn't explicitly state annual pricing, most trading platforms offer 15–20% discounts for annual commitments. At estimated annual rates, Pro would cost approximately $359/year (vs. $359.40 monthly), Premium around $599/year, and Elite roughly $959/year. Contact TraderSync directly to confirm current annual pricing.

Free Plan / Free Trial

TraderSync does not offer a free tier or free trial. This is a notable departure from some competitors and means there's an upfront commitment barrier.

For traders evaluating whether to commit, this represents a real risk—you're spending $29.95 to test the platform without a safety net. However, TraderSync's strong 4.4/5 rating and established user base (founded 2018) suggest most users find the entry-level Pro plan worth the cost.

Workaround: Many users recommend signing up for Pro ($29.95) for a single month to thoroughly test broker integration, mobile app performance, and whether the Cypher AI assistant matches your trading analysis style. If it doesn't fit, you're out $30 rather than locked into a longer commitment.

Hidden Costs and Fees

Beyond the base subscription, traders should budget for:

  • Broker Integration Fees: TraderSync integrates with major brokers (Interactive Brokers, TD Ameritrade, Alpaca, Webull, etc.), but some brokers charge data feed fees independent of TraderSync. Real-time stock data feeds can add $5–$20/month; real-time options data adds another $10–$15/month. These are broker costs, not TraderSync costs, but they compound your total trading infrastructure expense.
  • Market Replay Data: The Premium and Elite plans include market replay, but downloading historical intraday data for extended backtesting may incur bandwidth or historical data charges depending on your broker.
  • Tax Reporting Add-On: TraderSync generates trade-by-trade reports, but advanced tax optimization reports or third-party tax integration are not explicitly mentioned in base pricing. Clarify with support if you need automated wash-sale flagging or tax-loss harvesting data.
  • Mobile App: Included in all tiers—no separate app fee.
  • AI Message Limits (Pro tier): The Pro plan includes "limited" Cypher AI messages. Once exceeded, additional AI analysis queries may require an upgrade or a per-query fee. This is worth testing during your trial month.

TraderSync Pricing vs Competitors

How does TraderSync stack up against other trading journal platforms?

Platform Entry Price Top Tier Price Free Trial? AI Features
TraderSync $29.95/mo $79.95/mo No Cypher AI (conversational)
Edgewonk ~$25/mo* ~$60/mo* 7-day trial Basic pattern recognition
Thinkorswim + Custom Journal ~$0 (TD Ameritrade) Variable Free (broker-dependent) Limited AI; manual logging
Journify ~$15/mo* ~$45/mo* Free tier + trial Basic AI summaries

* Approximate pricing based on publicly available data as of May 2026.

TraderSync's Competitive Position:

  • Higher Entry Price: TraderSync's $29.95 starting point is above budget options (Journify ~$15) but justified by the Cypher AI assistant, which is more advanced than basic pattern recognition tools.
  • No Free Trial Friction: Edgewonk and Journify offer free trials or freemium tiers, which remove upfront risk. TraderSync's lack of free access is a competitive weakness, though offset by brand reputation and AI capabilities.
  • AI-First Advantage: Cypher AI sets TraderSync apart from older platforms like Edgewonk. Conversational trade analysis beats manual annotation for busy traders.
  • Multi-Account Support: Premium ($49.95) supports up to 10 accounts; Elite ($79.95) supports 20. Competitors typically limit multi-account access to higher tiers or charge per account. TraderSync's 10-account middle tier is generous.
  • Market Replay: Premium and above include 1-second precision market replay, a feature most competitors charge extra for or don't offer at all.

Is TraderSync Worth the Price?

For Day Traders & Prop Firm Traders: Strong Yes

The Pro plan ($29.95/mo) is a no-brainer for anyone serious about trading. At ~$360/year, it's less than a single losing trade for most day traders. The combination of structured journaling, broker integration, and Cypher AI analysis pays for itself within days if you act on insights. Upgrade to Premium ($49.95/mo) if you manage multiple accounts or rely heavily on market replay for reviewing entries.

For Options Traders: Yes, Consider Premium or Elite

Options trading involves complex position management and multi-leg strategies. The Premium tier ($49.95/mo) unlocks advanced analytics and unlimited Cypher AI messages, which are critical when analyzing spreads, volatility skew, and extrinsic value decay. For traders managing 5+ accounts or running strategies across different brokers, Premium is worth the upgrade.

For Swing Traders or Hobbyists: Maybe Pro

If you trade 1–5 times per week and don't need real-time market replay, Pro ($29.95/mo) works. However, the AI message limits may frustrate you. A single month of testing is strongly recommended before committing.

For Crypto Traders: Yes, Pro or Premium

TraderSync supports crypto (mentioned in Markets), and the ability to log cross-exchange trades and analyze them with Cypher AI is valuable given crypto's volatility. Start with Pro; upgrade to Premium if you trade multiple coins or use leverage.

For Prop Firm Traders: Elite ($79.95/mo) Required

The Elite tier's 20-account support is designed for prop traders managing multiple accounts under a firm's capital. The $79.95/mo cost is negligible compared to profit splits or minimum capital requirements. Unlimited Cypher AI messages are a massive productivity gain when analyzing dozens of daily trades.

How to Save on TraderSync

  • Annual Billing Discount: Confirm annual pricing with TraderSync support. Estimated savings: 15–20%, or ~$60–$120/year depending on plan.
  • Start with Pro, Upgrade Later: Don't lock into Elite immediately. Test Pro for 1–2 months, then upgrade if you hit AI message limits or need multi-account support. Unused features are wasted money.
  • Negotiate Team Pricing: If you're part of a trading group or prop firm with 3+ users, reach out to TraderSync sales about bulk discounts. Team seats often have 20–30% discounts.
  • Combine with Free/Low-Cost Tools: Use free charting platforms (TradingView free tier, Thinkorswim) for live analysis. Use TraderSync purely as a journaling + AI analysis tool, which maximizes ROI on your subscription.
  • Leverage Broker Rebates: Some brokers (Interactive Brokers, Alpaca) offer software discounts or rebates. Check if your broker has a TraderSync partnership.
  • Tax Deduction: If you're a self-employed trader or run an LLC, trading journal software is a legitimate business expense. The full subscription cost is tax-deductible, effectively reducing its real cost by your marginal tax rate (e.g., 30% for most professionals).

Final Verdict

TraderSync's pricing is fair for the features delivered, especially the Cypher AI assistant and market replay. The lack of a free trial is a friction point, but the platform's 4.4/5 rating and 2018 founding date signal stability. For active traders, $29.95–$79.95/mo is a small cost relative to trading P&L.

Recommendation: Sign up for Pro ($29.95/mo) for one month. If you love the UI and use Cypher AI daily, upgrade to Premium ($49.95/mo). Only move to Elite if you trade multiple accounts or run a team.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
